outlook_paragraph: Continental Resources shows zero active drilling sites at decision date, with no newly or continuously active pads, indicating a pause in field-level activity heading into Q2 2022. Despite elevated WTI averages near $88/bbl, the lack of current drilling momentum and only partial exposure to active segments (40% segment fraction) suggest revenue will be driven primarily by legacy production and pricing realizations. The deterministic forecast of $1.69B reflects this subdued operational backdrop, with revenue sustained more by favorable oil prices than near-term volume growth.
